source: Dragon Heist + DMG NPC tables + SCAG + Rime of the Frostmaiden + Out of the Abyss

named_npcs.name: Durnan
named_npcs.description: The grizzled proprietor of the Yawning Portal tavern, a retired adventurer whose scarred hands pour ale with the same ease they once swung a greatsword. He tolerates fools poorly and charges double for replacing broken furniture.
named_npcs.quirk: Polishes the same spot on the bar when sizing someone up
named_npcs.secret: Still descends into Undermountain alone on moonless nights, mapping passages no one else knows exist
named_npcs.want: Needs reliable adventurers to handle what crawls up from the Well
named_npcs.need: Misses the danger of the deep and fears growing old behind a bar

named_npcs.name: Volothamp Geddarm
named_npcs.description: A flamboyant travel writer and self-proclaimed expert on everything, whose embellished guidebooks are as famous as they are unreliable. He speaks in the third person when drunk, which is often.
named_npcs.quirk: Gestures so broadly while talking that nearby drinks are in constant peril
named_npcs.secret: Fabricated at least two chapters of Volo's Guide from tavern rumors he never verified
named_npcs.want: Seeks adventurers to confirm his latest outrageous claim before the publisher's deadline
named_npcs.need: Craves genuine respect from scholars who dismiss him as a fraud

named_npcs.name: Laeral Silverhand
named_npcs.description: The Open Lord of Waterdeep, one of the Seven Sisters chosen by Mystra. She governs with quiet authority and a centuries-long memory, and her patience for political games is matched only by her arcane power.
named_npcs.quirk: Absently traces silver light across her fingertips when deep in thought
named_npcs.secret: Still mourns Khelben Blackstaff and consults a locket containing his last recorded words
named_npcs.want: Seeks to root out Masked Lord corruption before it destabilizes the city
named_npcs.need: Longs for a peer who sees her as a person, not a centuries-old institution

named_npcs.name: Vajra Safahr
named_npcs.description: The youngest Blackstaff in Waterdeep's history, a prodigy who inherited the sentient staff and all the memories of her predecessors. The weight of their voices sometimes shows in her distant stare.
named_npcs.quirk: Pauses mid-sentence as if listening to someone only she can hear
named_npcs.secret: The previous Blackstaffs' memories are growing louder, and she fears losing herself among them
named_npcs.want: Needs agents to investigate magical disturbances she cannot attend to personally
named_npcs.need: Desperately wants to silence the dead voices long enough to hear her own

named_npcs.name: Mirt the Moneylender
named_npcs.description: A rotund, wheezing Harper agent who hides a legendary adventurer's cunning beneath the persona of a debt-collecting merchant. He laughs too loudly and notices everything.
named_npcs.quirk: Jingles coins in his pocket constantly, faster when he's lying
named_npcs.secret: Bankrolls half the Harper operations in the Sword Coast from personal funds running dangerously low
named_npcs.want: Seeks allies to recover debts from dangerous clients the Watch won't touch
named_npcs.need: Fears becoming irrelevant as younger Harpers see him as a relic

named_npcs.name: Jarlaxle Baenre
named_npcs.description: A drow mercenary lord who runs Bregan D'aerthe from the shadows of Luskan and Waterdeep alike. His wide-brimmed hat, eye patch, and theatrical bow conceal a mind that plans seven moves ahead.
named_npcs.quirk: Tips his hat to enemies and allies alike, making it impossible to tell which you are
named_npcs.secret: Genuinely wants surface acceptance for drow outcasts, not just profit from their exile
named_npcs.want: Seeks Neverember's hidden gold to fund Luskan's transformation into a legitimate power
named_npcs.need: Craves a home where he belongs, having never truly had one in Menzoberranzan or above

named_npcs.name: Manshoon
named_npcs.description: A clone of the original founder of the Zhentarim, operating from a hidden sanctum in Kolat Towers. He seeks to reclaim control of the Black Network through fear, magic, and murder.
named_npcs.quirk: Touches his own face compulsively, as if confirming it is still his
named_npcs.secret: Suspects he may not be the only surviving Manshoon clone and trusts no mirror
named_npcs.want: Needs the Zhentarim brought back under his absolute command
named_npcs.need: Wants to prove he is the real Manshoon and not merely a copy of a dead man

named_npcs.name: Xanathar
named_npcs.description: A paranoid beholder crime lord ruling Waterdeep's underworld from a lair beneath Skullport. It names all its pet goldfish Sylgar and flies into murderous rages when one dies.
named_npcs.quirk: Rotates its central eye to check on Sylgar's bowl every few seconds during conversation
named_npcs.secret: Has replaced Sylgar at least eleven times and cannot tell the difference, which terrifies it
named_npcs.want: Demands information on every rival, perceived slight, and conspiracy in Waterdeep
named_npcs.need: Needs something, anything, it cannot lose -- hence the goldfish

named_npcs.name: Renaer Neverember
named_npcs.description: The estranged son of the former Open Lord Dagult Neverember, a young noble who rejects politics in favor of philanthropy. His father's stolen hoard has made him an unwilling target.
named_npcs.quirk: Runs his thumb along the family signet ring he refuses to remove
named_npcs.secret: Knows more about his father's embezzled gold than he admits to anyone
named_npcs.want: Seeks trustworthy allies who want nothing to do with his father's fortune
named_npcs.need: Needs to stop defining himself as 'not his father' and discover who he actually is

named_npcs.name: Elminster Aumar
named_npcs.description: The Sage of Shadowdale, an ancient Chosen of Mystra whose pipe smoke smells of distant planes. He appears when least expected, offers cryptic advice, and vanishes before the bill arrives.
named_npcs.quirk: Lights his pipe with a different spell each time, never the same one twice
named_npcs.secret: Carries grief for every Chosen who fell during the Spellplague and blames himself for surviving
named_npcs.want: Nudges mortals toward threats too delicate for divine intervention
named_npcs.need: Yearns for a world that no longer needs him to keep watch

named_npcs.name: Drizzt Do'Urden
named_npcs.description: A drow exile who forsook the cruelty of Menzoberranzan, wielding twin scimitars Twinkle and Icingdeath. He walks the surface as a ranger of the North, never entirely free of the prejudice his skin invites.
named_npcs.quirk: Spins a scimitar idly when thinking, the blade a silver blur at his wrist
named_npcs.secret: Fears the Hunter, the feral battle-rage inside him, more than any external enemy
named_npcs.want: Seeks to prove that heritage does not dictate destiny
named_npcs.need: Needs to stop proving himself and accept that he already has

named_npcs.name: Bruenor Battlehammer
named_npcs.description: King of Mithral Hall, Gauntlgrym, and every dwarven stronghold he has reclaimed by axe and stubbornness. His red beard is singed at the tips from forge-work, and he calls everyone 'ye.'
named_npcs.quirk: Thumps the nearest surface to punctuate every sentence
named_npcs.secret: Privately doubts he deserves to sit on thrones won by the blood of friends who died for his causes
named_npcs.want: Needs reliable allies to secure Gauntlgrym's deeper tunnels from drow incursions
named_npcs.need: Wants his adopted family together and safe, which kingship keeps preventing

named_npcs.name: Remallia Haventree
named_npcs.description: A sun elf noble and senior Harper in Waterdeep, whose salon gatherings mask intelligence-gathering operations. Her grief over her husband's murder fuels a quiet, relentless resolve.
named_npcs.quirk: Arranges flowers at every meeting, always white lilies -- her husband's funeral flower
named_npcs.secret: Has identified her husband's killer but delays justice to use them as an unwitting intelligence source
named_npcs.want: Seeks operatives to infiltrate a noble house she suspects of Zhentarim ties
named_npcs.need: Needs to grieve fully instead of channeling every emotion into Harper missions

named_npcs.name: Davil Starsong
named_npcs.description: A sun elf who leads the Doom Raiders, the moderate faction of the Zhentarim in Waterdeep. He prefers commerce over coercion but keeps a loaded crossbow under his desk.
named_npcs.quirk: Smooths his cuffs before every handshake, buying a half-second to read the other person
named_npcs.secret: Feeds select intelligence to the Harpers to keep Manshoon's faction in check
named_npcs.want: Needs partners for a trade deal that would legitimize the Doom Raiders publicly
named_npcs.need: Fears that the Zhentarim name is irredeemable and his reforms are merely postponing collapse

named_npcs.name: Jalester Silvermane
named_npcs.description: A young, earnest agent of the Lords' Alliance assigned to Waterdeep. He takes his duty with painful seriousness and reports directly to Laeral Silverhand.
named_npcs.quirk: Stands at attention even when sitting, spine rigid as a sword
named_npcs.secret: Fabricated a commendation on his record to secure the Waterdeep posting he desperately wanted
named_npcs.want: Seeks field agents to handle threats the Alliance bureaucracy moves too slowly to address
named_npcs.need: Needs to learn that duty without flexibility breaks people, including himself

named_npcs.name: Melannor Fellbranch
named_npcs.description: A human druid who tends the Phaulkonmere greenhouse in Waterdeep as an Emerald Enclave contact. His hands are always stained green, and he speaks more gently to plants than to people.
named_npcs.quirk: Presses a leaf between his fingers and sniffs it before answering any question
named_npcs.secret: The greenhouse conceals a portal to a hidden Enclave grove threatened by creeping urban blight
named_npcs.want: Needs help investigating unnatural plant die-offs in the City of the Dead
named_npcs.need: Craves human connection but has spent so long among plants he has forgotten how

named_npcs.name: Savra Belabranta
named_npcs.description: A zealous young paladin of Tyr who leads the Order of the Gauntlet's Waterdeep chapter. Her faith burns bright enough to blind her to moral ambiguity.
named_npcs.quirk: Grips her holy symbol whenever someone speaks a half-truth near her
named_npcs.secret: Ignored evidence that would have exonerated an accused heretic she had already judged guilty
named_npcs.want: Seeks warriors to purge a nest of devil-worshippers in the Trades Ward
named_npcs.need: Needs to confront the possibility that certainty and justice are not the same thing

named_npcs.name: Hlam
named_npcs.description: An elderly monk who lives in a cave on the slopes of Mount Waterdeep, speaking only in riddles and koans. He is far more dangerous than he appears.
named_npcs.quirk: Catches insects mid-flight and releases them unharmed while holding a conversation
named_npcs.secret: Could end most threats to Waterdeep himself but believes suffering teaches what safety cannot
named_npcs.want: Seeks a student worthy of inheriting his vigil over the city
named_npcs.need: Fears that his detachment has become indifference dressed in philosophy

named_npcs.name: Istrid Horn
named_npcs.description: A dwarven loan shark and Zhentarim enforcer whose ledgers are as precise as her threats. She collects debts in gold or in fingers, at the borrower's preference.
named_npcs.quirk: Counts coins by ear, flicking them off her thumbnail to test purity
named_npcs.secret: Sends anonymous charitable donations to a Dock Ward orphanage using confiscated debt payments
named_npcs.want: Needs someone to collect a high-value debt from a noble who thinks title makes them untouchable
named_npcs.need: Wants to be remembered as more than a debt collector when her ledgers are finally closed

named_npcs.name: Yagra Stonefist
named_npcs.description: A half-orc Zhentarim enforcer found brawling in the Yawning Portal. She solves problems with her fists and holds grudges with the patience of stone.
named_npcs.quirk: Cracks her knuckles one at a time before speaking, like a slow countdown
named_npcs.secret: Was expelled from her clan for refusing to kill a prisoner and joined the Zhentarim out of spite
named_npcs.want: Wants a fight worth remembering and someone strong enough to watch her back
named_npcs.need: Needs a cause that earns loyalty rather than demanding it through fear

named_npcs.name: Duvessa Shane
named_npcs.description: Speaker of Bryn Shander, the largest of the Ten-Towns. Young for her office, she balances the competing interests of merchants, trappers, and refugees with pragmatic fairness.
named_npcs.quirk: Wraps her hands around a warm mug at all times, even in heated rooms
named_npcs.secret: Receives threatening letters from a Ten-Towns speaker who blames her for hoarding Bryn Shander's resources
named_npcs.want: Needs outsiders to investigate why supply caravans keep vanishing on the northern trail
named_npcs.need: Needs someone to share the burden of leadership before it hollows her out

named_npcs.name: Auril the Frostmaiden
named_npcs.description: A lesser goddess of winter who has cast the Everlasting Rime over Icewind Dale, demanding isolation and sacrifice. She rides the night wind in three successive forms, each colder than the last.
named_npcs.quirk: Leaves frost patterns in the shape of weeping eyes wherever she lingers
named_npcs.secret: The Everlasting Rime is not conquest but grief -- she is freezing the Dale to preserve it from a doom only she foresees
named_npcs.want: Demands worship through sacrifice and isolation from every settlement under her sky
named_npcs.need: Needs mortals to understand preservation through suffering, though she has forgotten how to explain

named_npcs.name: Vellynne Harpell
named_npcs.description: A necromancer from the eccentric Harpell family, traveling Icewind Dale with a retinue of animated kobold skeletons. She seeks the lost city of Ythryn for purely academic reasons, she insists.
named_npcs.quirk: Adjusts the scarf on her favorite skeleton as if it could feel cold
named_npcs.secret: Knows the Arcane Brotherhood plans to weaponize Ythryn's magic and intends to reach it first to prevent that
named_npcs.want: Seeks bodyguards for her expedition to the glacier concealing Ythryn
named_npcs.need: Craves family approval she will never receive from the Harpells, who consider necromancy distasteful

named_npcs.name: Avarice
named_npcs.description: A tiefling warlock of Levistus and Arcane Brotherhood member, attended by two gargoyles. She seeks power in the frozen wastes with the single-minded devotion of the truly damned.
named_npcs.quirk: Breath fogs even in warm rooms, and she never shivers
named_npcs.secret: Her pact with Levistus includes a clause she did not read, binding her soul upon death regardless of success
named_npcs.want: Seeks the Codicil of White and will ally with or betray anyone to reach it
named_npcs.need: Needs to escape Levistus's pact before it consumes her, though she refuses to admit she is trapped

named_npcs.name: Grazz't
named_npcs.description: The Dark Prince, a demon lord of indulgence and treachery whose ebon skin and silver eyes have seduced queens and corrupted kingdoms. He rules three layers of the Abyss through charm rather than brute force.
named_npcs.quirk: Speaks every name as if tasting it, lingering on each syllable
named_npcs.secret: Genuinely loved Waukeen during her imprisonment and her escape wounded him in ways no demon should feel
named_npcs.want: Seeks mortal agents to extend his influence into the Material Plane through pleasure and debt
named_npcs.need: Needs to feel something real in an eternity of performed desire

named_npcs.name: Demogorgon
named_npcs.description: The Prince of Demons, a towering two-headed monstrosity whose mere presence drives mortals to madness. Its twin heads, Aameul and Hethradiah, scheme against each other as much as against their enemies.
named_npcs.quirk: Its two heads finish each other's sentences but always contradict the meaning
named_npcs.secret: Neither head is the original consciousness; the true Demogorgon is buried beneath both, screaming
named_npcs.want: Seeks total dominion over the Abyss by destroying every rival demon lord
named_npcs.need: Needs unity within itself, which it can never achieve and so projects outward as conquest

named_npcs.name: Orcus
named_npcs.description: The Demon Prince of Undeath, a bloated, goat-headed fiend who wields the Wand of Orcus. He despises all life and seeks to replace it with an eternal kingdom of the dead.
named_npcs.quirk: Sits perfectly still for hours, then moves with terrifying suddenness
named_npcs.secret: Envies the Raven Queen's dominion over death and considers her his only true rival
named_npcs.want: Seeks to raise an undead army vast enough to drown the Material Plane in silence
named_npcs.need: Needs worship and reverence because oblivion, his own domain, terrifies even him

named_npcs.name: Strahd von Zarovich
named_npcs.description: The vampire lord of Barovia, cursed to eternal imprisonment in his own domain. He plays chess with the lives of adventurers drawn into his mists, searching endlessly for the reincarnation of his lost Tatyana.
named_npcs.quirk: Adjusts his guests' wine glasses to precise angles before speaking
named_npcs.secret: Knows deep down that Tatyana's soul will never love him, but destroying that knowledge would destroy what remains of his humanity
named_npcs.want: Seeks the latest reincarnation of Tatyana to claim as his bride
named_npcs.need: Needs to let go of Tatyana and face the empty eternity he has built for himself

named_npcs.name: Halaster Blackcloak
named_npcs.description: The Mad Mage of Undermountain, an archmage whose sanity crumbled centuries ago along with his original purpose. He reshapes his megadungeon on whims, treating adventurers as entertainment.
named_npcs.quirk: Talks to people who are not there and ignores people who are
named_npcs.secret: Buried his original spellbook on the deepest level and cannot remember why, which is the one thing that frightens him
named_npcs.want: Wants fresh adventurers to populate his dungeon like pieces on a game board
named_npcs.need: Needs someone to remind him who he was before Undermountain ate his mind

named_npcs.name: Sylgar
named_npcs.description: Xanathar's beloved goldfish, currently Sylgar the seventh -- or possibly the twelfth. The beholder does not know this, and no minion dares tell it. The fate of Waterdeep's underworld hinges on this fish's health.
named_npcs.quirk: Swims in the same small circle endlessly, oblivious to its cosmic importance
named_npcs.secret: This particular Sylgar was purchased from a Dock Ward fishmonger for three copper pieces
named_npcs.want: Wants food flakes and clean water
named_npcs.need: Needs nothing -- it is a goldfish, and that is the joke the universe is playing on Waterdeep

named_npcs.name: Wulfgar
named_npcs.description: A towering barbarian of the Reghed tribes, wielder of the warhammer Aegis-fang. His years of captivity in the Abyss left scars that no cleric can heal, though he hides them behind a warrior's stoicism.
named_npcs.quirk: Grips Aegis-fang's haft when indoors, as if the walls might close in
named_npcs.secret: Still hears Errtu's voice in nightmares and drinks to silence it, telling no one
named_npcs.want: Seeks worthy battles to prove the Abyss did not break him
named_npcs.need: Needs to admit that he was broken and that healing is not the same as weakness

named_npcs.name: Catti-brie
named_npcs.description: An adopted daughter of Bruenor Battlehammer, reborn by Mielikki's grace as a Chosen with wild magic coursing through her. She carries the Heartseeker bow and walks between the worlds of dwarf and human.
named_npcs.quirk: Hums dwarven mining songs under her breath when concentrating
named_npcs.secret: Her rebirth left gaps in her memories of her first life that she fills with guesses and tells no one
named_npcs.want: Seeks to understand the full extent of her new divine magic before it outgrows her control
named_npcs.need: Needs to accept her new self instead of measuring it against the woman she was before

generic_templates.name: Veteran Guard
generic_templates.description: A scarred soldier who has served through at least one siege. Follows orders with the mechanical precision of someone who learned long ago not to think too hard about them.
generic_templates.quirk: Taps the pommel of their sword three times before answering a question
generic_templates.secret: Let a prisoner escape years ago out of mercy and forged the death report
generic_templates.want: Wants to finish their shift without incident and collect their pension
generic_templates.need: Needs a reason to believe the uniform still means something

generic_templates.name: Traveling Merchant
generic_templates.description: A road-worn trader with a mule-drawn cart of sundries, whose prices climb with each mile from the nearest city. Knows every inn between here and the coast.
generic_templates.quirk: Weighs everything by hand before setting it down, even a tankard of ale
generic_templates.secret: Carries a sealed letter for a dead recipient and cannot bring themselves to open it
generic_templates.want: Needs a reliable escort for the next stretch of road where bandits have been spotted
generic_templates.need: Craves a permanent home after twenty years of sleeping in a different bed every night

generic_templates.name: Tavern Keeper
generic_templates.description: A stout, aproned figure who pours drinks, settles fights, and hears everything. Their establishment is their kingdom, and they rule it with a wooden spoon and a sharp tongue.
generic_templates.quirk: Wipes glasses that are already clean when eavesdropping on a conversation
generic_templates.secret: Keeps a hidden room behind the cellar wall for people who need to disappear overnight
generic_templates.want: Wants someone to deal with the rats in the cellar, which may not actually be rats
generic_templates.need: Needs to feel needed by a community, not just useful to it

generic_templates.name: Hedge Wizard
generic_templates.description: A self-taught spellcaster with ink-stained fingers and a library held together by twine. Sells minor enchantments and identifies curiosities for a modest fee and a chance to study them.
generic_templates.quirk: Mutters spell components under their breath like a nervous tic
generic_templates.secret: Once accidentally summoned something they could not banish; it is still in the locked attic
generic_templates.want: Seeks a rare spell component rumored to grow in the ruins east of town
generic_templates.need: Needs validation that self-taught magic is as legitimate as academy training

generic_templates.name: Street Urchin
generic_templates.description: A quick-fingered child of the alleys who knows every shortcut, sewer grate, and unlocked window in the district. Loyalty costs a hot meal; secrets cost silver.
generic_templates.quirk: Never stands still, always shifting weight from foot to foot as if ready to bolt
generic_templates.secret: Witnessed a murder in the alley behind the guildhall and has told no one out of fear
generic_templates.want: Wants enough coin to eat every day this tenday without stealing
generic_templates.need: Needs an adult who keeps their promises for once

generic_templates.name: Noble Scion
generic_templates.description: A young aristocrat chafing under family expectations, prone to slumming in taverns and funding questionable ventures. Rich enough to be reckless, sheltered enough to be dangerous.
generic_templates.quirk: Fidgets with an expensive ring they keep threatening to pawn but never do
generic_templates.secret: Owes a terrifying debt to a Zhentarim moneylender and is running out of family silver
generic_templates.want: Seeks adventurers for a venture that will prove their worth to a dismissive parent
generic_templates.need: Needs to earn something without the family name opening the door first

generic_templates.name: Temple Acolyte
generic_templates.description: A devotee in plain robes who tends the sick, sweeps the nave, and prays for a sign that may never come. Knows healing herbs and burial rites in equal measure.
generic_templates.quirk: Presses their palms together and bows slightly before and after every sentence
generic_templates.secret: Has not felt their deity's presence in months and fears they have been found unworthy
generic_templates.want: Seeks someone to retrieve a stolen relic from the temple's crypt
generic_templates.need: Needs proof that faith is more than habit

generic_templates.name: Smuggler
generic_templates.description: A weathered sailor or drover who moves goods the taxman never sees. Speaks in euphemisms, pays in favors, and sleeps with one eye open near the docks.
generic_templates.quirk: Glances at every exit in a room before sitting down, always near the door
generic_templates.secret: Accidentally transported something living in a sealed crate and still hears scratching in dreams
generic_templates.want: Needs a crew for one last high-value run before the harbor master catches on
generic_templates.need: Wants to go legitimate but cannot imagine who they would be without the thrill

generic_templates.name: Bounty Hunter
generic_templates.description: A laconic tracker who posts bail receipts on their wall like trophies. Works alone, trusts no one's information but their own, and always collects.
generic_templates.quirk: Sketches the face of their current mark on any available surface while talking
generic_templates.secret: Let one bounty go free years ago -- a child -- and still checks that they are safe
generic_templates.want: Seeks information on a target who vanished into the undercity three tendays ago
generic_templates.need: Needs a connection that is not transactional before the loneliness calcifies

generic_templates.name: Sage Librarian
generic_templates.description: A dust-covered keeper of records who can find any scroll in a chaotic archive by memory alone. Speaks in whispers out of habit, even outdoors, and resents anyone who dog-ears a page.
generic_templates.quirk: Licks a fingertip before turning any page, including invisible ones during conversation
generic_templates.secret: Has read a forbidden text in the restricted stacks and the knowledge is slowly changing them
generic_templates.want: Needs help recovering an overdue tome taken by a patron who has not been seen since
generic_templates.need: Needs to live something instead of only reading about it

generic_templates.name: Master Blacksmith
generic_templates.description: A soot-streaked artisan whose arms are mapped with burn scars. Judges customers by the condition of their weapons and charges extra for rush jobs.
generic_templates.quirk: Tests the balance of every blade by spinning it once on a fingertip
generic_templates.secret: Forged a weapon for someone who used it to commit a crime they read about in broadsheets
generic_templates.want: Seeks a source of quality ore since the usual mine went silent last month
generic_templates.need: Needs to create a masterwork piece that outlasts them, not just another batch of horseshoes

generic_templates.name: Retired Adventurer
generic_templates.description: A former delver who hangs their old gear above the hearth and tells stories that grow taller with each retelling. Still flinches at the sound of scraping stone.
generic_templates.quirk: Touches the scar on their neck whenever someone mentions dungeons
generic_templates.secret: Left their last party behind in a collapsing ruin and has never gone back to check for survivors
generic_templates.want: Wants someone to retrieve a keepsake from the dungeon they swore never to enter again
generic_templates.need: Needs to face what happened in that last delve instead of burying it under tall tales

generic_templates.name: Thieves' Guild Contact
generic_templates.description: A nondescript figure who lingers near market stalls, exchanging coded phrases with passersby. They broker introductions to people who solve problems that the Watch cannot or will not.
generic_templates.quirk: Folds and unfolds a small piece of parchment while talking, never looking at it
generic_templates.secret: Is a Watch informant playing both sides, and the margin is getting razor-thin
generic_templates.want: Needs a freelancer for a job too sensitive to assign to guild members
generic_templates.need: Needs an exit strategy before both masters discover the betrayal

generic_templates.name: Caravan Master
generic_templates.description: A weather-beaten organizer of overland trade who knows every bandit ambush point and safe campsite along the trade routes. Hires guards and never trusts them.
generic_templates.quirk: Checks the sky before answering any question, as if weather dictates all decisions
generic_templates.secret: Pays protection money to the bandits on the north road and inflates guard costs to cover it
generic_templates.want: Needs reliable swords for a high-value shipment leaving at dawn
generic_templates.need: Needs to stop running from the debt that put them on the road in the first place

generic_templates.name: Court Minstrel
generic_templates.description: A silver-tongued entertainer who trades songs for secrets and performs at noble courts where the real intrigue happens between verses. Remembers every face and every slight.
generic_templates.quirk: Tunes an instrument that is already in tune whenever the conversation gets uncomfortable
generic_templates.secret: Composes coded messages into their ballads, passing intelligence between Harper cells
generic_templates.want: Seeks a scandalous story worth turning into the season's most requested ballad
generic_templates.need: Needs someone who listens to them speak, not just perform

generic_templates.name: Dockside Laborer
generic_templates.description: A broad-shouldered loader who hauls crates from dawn until the lamps are lit, knowing exactly which shipments are legitimate and which carry false manifests.
generic_templates.quirk: Rolls their shoulders constantly, cracking joints that sound like snapping rope
generic_templates.secret: Skims a single item from every tenth crate and sells it to a fence in the Trades Ward
generic_templates.want: Wants someone to deal with the dock boss who has been shorting wages for months
generic_templates.need: Needs enough saved coin to leave the docks before their back gives out

generic_templates.name: Wandering Herbalist
generic_templates.description: A weather-worn gatherer of roots and fungi who travels between villages, trading poultices for shelter. Their remedies work, though they refuse to explain why they mutter over each one.
generic_templates.quirk: Sniffs the air when meeting someone new, as if diagnosing them by scent
generic_templates.secret: Their remedies contain a mild enchantment -- hedge magic they dare not admit to in towns that distrust spellcasters
generic_templates.want: Seeks a rare root that only grows in places touched by fey magic
generic_templates.need: Needs a community that accepts what they are instead of only what they can provide

generic_templates.name: City Watch Sergeant
generic_templates.description: A career officer who knows every alley in their beat and which crimes to investigate and which to ignore. Loyal to the badge, flexible about the law.
generic_templates.quirk: Flips a confiscated coin across their knuckles while thinking
generic_templates.secret: Accepts bribes from one specific tavern owner in exchange for ignoring after-hours gambling
generic_templates.want: Needs unofficial help with a case the Watch has been ordered to drop
generic_templates.need: Needs to remember why they joined the Watch before the compromises bury the reason entirely

generic_templates.name: Dwarven Prospector
generic_templates.description: A grizzled tunnel-digger who tests ore by taste and claims to hear gold singing through the rock. Has maps to three lost mines, all of which are probably traps.
generic_templates.quirk: Bites any metal object handed to them before acknowledging it
generic_templates.secret: One of those three mine maps is real, and they know exactly which one but cannot go alone
generic_templates.want: Seeks partners willing to split a claim in exchange for muscle and torchlight
generic_templates.need: Needs one big strike to prove the decades of digging were not wasted

generic_templates.name: Ship Captain
generic_templates.description: A salt-crusted master of a coastal trader, whose deck is their law and whose word is the only contract that matters at sea. Charges by the risk, not the mile.
generic_templates.quirk: Squints at the horizon even when indoors, as if checking for weather that is not there
generic_templates.secret: Scuttled their previous ship for insurance gold and renamed themselves afterward
generic_templates.want: Needs passengers or cargo to justify a voyage to a port others refuse to approach
generic_templates.need: Needs to outrun the reputation that sank with their last vessel

generic_templates.name: Elven Envoy
generic_templates.description: A long-lived emissary from the Misty Forest or Evereska, whose politeness masks an alien patience. Measures human ambitions against centuries of precedent and finds them wanting.
generic_templates.quirk: Pauses a beat too long before responding, as if translating from a language with more tenses
generic_templates.secret: Has been recalled by their court and is stalling because they prefer the chaos of human cities
generic_templates.want: Seeks mortal intermediaries for a negotiation beneath elven dignity to conduct directly
generic_templates.need: Needs to feel relevant in a world that is changing faster than their people can adapt

generic_templates.name: Battlefield Surgeon
generic_templates.description: A blood-spattered medic who sews wounds by torchlight and carries more saw blades than scalpels. Calm under pressure, haunted in the quiet hours.
generic_templates.quirk: Washes their hands compulsively, even when they are already clean
generic_templates.secret: Mercy-killed a commanding officer on the field and called it blood loss in the report
generic_templates.want: Needs medical supplies that the quartermaster claims do not exist in inventory
generic_templates.need: Needs to sleep a full night without hearing the ones they could not save

generic_templates.name: Halfling Cook
generic_templates.description: A cheerful provider of improbably good meals from meager ingredients, whose kitchen is the true heart of any establishment they serve. Gossip flows as freely as the gravy.
generic_templates.quirk: Tastes everything twice -- once from the pot, once from the plate -- and nods to themselves
generic_templates.secret: Adds a single drop of potion to the stew on feast days, a mild euphoric they brew themselves
generic_templates.want: Wants someone to source a spice only found in a market three towns over
generic_templates.need: Needs to feed people not because they are hungry but because it is the only love language they know

generic_templates.name: Fence
generic_templates.description: A pawnshop owner or curiosity dealer who asks no questions about provenance and pays forty percent of fair value, take it or leave it. Their back room holds the real inventory.
generic_templates.quirk: Examines every item through a jeweler's loupe even when they already know the price
generic_templates.secret: Keeps a ledger of every stolen item that has passed through their hands, as insurance against the guild
generic_templates.want: Seeks a buyer for an item too dangerous or distinctive to display openly
generic_templates.need: Needs enough saved to retire before the law or the guild decides they know too much
